title: Finding
description: A single issue surfaced by a reviewer or validator. Designed for clustering, metrics, and self-learning.

schema_version.description: Schema version. Bump on breaking field changes; orchestrator switches by version.

id.description: Server-minted on ingest (f-<YYYY-MM-DD>-<random6>). Agents do NOT emit this — any value supplied is ignored. Minting server-side makes finding ids collision-free across parallel reviewers.

task_id.description: The canonical task_id this finding belongs to.

file.description: Path relative to repo root, or null when finding is not file-bound (e.g. plan-level issue)

category.description: Controlled vocab — must match an entry in category-vocab.json for the finding's agent. If a new pattern is being recorded, use 'other' and populate proposed_new_category.

proposed_new_category.description: When category='other', describe the proposed new vocab entry. Reviewed via /agent-feedback before being added to vocab.

pattern_id.description: When this finding matches a known past-miss pattern: <agent>:<category>:<short-slug>. null if novel.

summary.description: One-line description of the issue. <=200 chars.

evidence_excerpt.description: Short code excerpt anchoring the finding. Trim to <=400 chars.

suggested_fix.description: Concrete proposed fix or pointer to an approach. <=300 chars.

ref_rule_id.description: When the finding cites a senior-pattern reference rule (e.g. 'redis.md#hot-keys'), include the anchor for traceability.
